articles.title: MSCI Delays Indonesia’s Market Status Review Until November
articles.summary: MSCI Inc. again decided to postpone its review on Indonesian equities, saying it needs more time to see whether recently announced transparency reforms are effective.

articles.title: Nomura Joins Sony in Powering Big Week for Japan Bond Sales
articles.summary: Nomura Holdings Inc. and Sony Group Corp. pushed bond sales by Japanese firms in global markets to more than $8 billion in the first two days of the week, in what is already one of the busiest periods

articles.title: Asian Stocks Rise After Chip-Fueled Global Selloff: Markets Wrap
articles.summary: Stocks in Asia rose in early trading after a global tech-led selloff on Tuesday that renewed concerns that the artificial intelligence-driven equity rally may have run too far.

articles.title: Musk’s SpaceX Adds Billions in Debt While Cutting Interest Costs
articles.summary: Elon Musk is no stranger to financial alchemy. His latest feat: adding billions of dollars of debt onto his cash-burning business empire while simultaneously cutting its annual interest burden.

articles.title: Oil Extends Drop as More Tankers Cross Hormuz After Peace Talks

articles.title: Pimco Sees China’s Export Glut Boosting Emerging-Market Bonds
articles.summary: China’s flood of cheap exports is bolstering the case for emerging-market bonds by helping to keep inflation under control across the developing world, according to Pacific Investment Management Co.

articles.title: Gold Drops Below $4,100 as Tech-Led Selloff Spurs Liquidation
articles.summary: Gold extended a decline, as a tech-led selloff on Wall Street prompted investors to cut bullion holdings to cover losses elsewhere in their portfolios.

articles.title: MSCI Maintains Korea’s Emerging Market Status in Latest Review
articles.summary: MSCI Inc. kept South Korea in its emerging-market index and refrained from starting a formal review process that could eventually elevate the $5 trillion stock market to developed-market status.
